output.dependency: The location/label field (e.g., a textual origin like a city or dataset segment name) is semantically linked to the route/flight identifier field, because the same route code appears alongside the origin label in the records.

Two time-of-day fields appear to be ordered stages of the same event/process (e.g., scheduled vs another milestone). Their values are consistent within a record and follow a typical chronological progression, suggesting one time derives from or is constrained by the other.

The earliest time field and a later time field are both tied to the same route/event and are likely related via a fixed elapsed-time or scheduling structure (departure-to-arrival style). This creates a semantic/scheduling dependency between the two time columns.

The earliest time field and another later time field (another milestone/estimate) are tied to the same route/event. Their consistent pairing implies the later time is determined by the earlier time plus an itinerary-dependent offset.

A middle time field and a later time field are chronologically and structurally related (milestone sequencing for the same route/event), indicating one is constrained by the other within the schedule/itinerary.
Another milestone/estimate time field is dependent on the middle time field through the same event itinerary sequencing, implying a structured time progression between them.

Two later time fields likely represent different types of end-stage times (e.g., planned vs actual or arrival vs another completion milestone). Their values are linked within the same route/event, indicating one determines or is constrained by the other.
